Long ago, the bat was a bird with feathers of brilliant gold, but he was vain and flew only during the day so the Sun could admire him. One afternoon, he mocked the Moon, calling her a pale ghost unworthy of the sky.

Insulted, the Moon cursed him. "Since you love your reflection so much, you shall never see it again. I take your sight and your golden feathers."

Instantly, the bat turned grey and leathery. His eyes clouded over, and the world went dark. Panic seized him as he fluttered blindly, crashing into trees and cliffs. He cried out in terror, a high-pitched shriek of despair.
